title: Application Log Corruption Due to Concurrent File Access
symptoms: Log files containing partial lines and binary garbage characters
ElasticSearch queries returning malformed log entries
Fluentd error logs showing parse failures on corrupted data
Logrotate and Fluentd competing for file handles on same files
Kibana dashboards showing malformed log visualization
root_cause: Race condition between Fluentd log shipper and logrotate process both accessing the same log files simultaneously without file locking or coordination
resolution: Implemented Fluentd-native log rotation to replace system logrotate, cleared corrupted entries from ElasticSearch index, and re-ingested logs from application-level buffers where available
preventive_actions: Migrate to Fluentd-native log rotation for all managed log files
Implement log integrity verification in the shipping pipeline
Add file locking mechanism for shared log file access
Create log corruption detection alerting in ElasticSearch
Implement application-level log buffering for recovery scenarios

timeline.event: Log corruption begins when logrotate and Fluentd access files simultaneously
Fluentd parse errors spike in monitoring
Corrupted log entries accumulating in ElasticSearch
Alex Johnson notices corrupted entries during routine log review
Root cause identified as Fluentd/logrotate race condition
Corrupted ElasticSearch entries tagged for cleanup
Log pipeline stabilized, new entries clean

embedding_summary: Application log corruption on log-aggregator-prod-02 caused by race condition between Fluentd log shipper and logrotate process accessing same files simultaneously. 2 hours of corrupted logs across 8 services contaminated ElasticSearch index. Resolved by migrating to Fluentd-native log rotation.
